transcript_facts:
- [Findings] uh ... looks like you've got a history of anaphylaxis , is that correct ?
- [Findings] so on exam you've got some tenderness over your lateral epicondyle .
- [Findings] uh , and you have some pain on the dorsal aspect of your- of your forearm as well . so let's look at your x-rays . hey dragon , show me the x-rays . your x-ray of your elbow- there's no fracture- it , uh , there's a little bit of swelling there on the lateral elbow but i do n't see any fracture , so that's good . so , looking at the x-ray and looking at your exam , uh , my diagnosis here would be lateral epicondylitis , and this is basically inflammation of this area where this tendon in- inserts .
- [History of Present Illness] and i've been taking ibuprofen , but it still hurts at lot .
- [History of Present Illness] do you take any medications for it ? um , ep- ... just an epipen . just epipen for anaphylaxis when you need it . um , and what surgeries have you had before ?
- [History of Present Illness] yeah , no , uh , your , uh , neck surgery .
- [History of Present Illness] and is it hurt- ... tender right around that area ?
- [History of Present Illness] so there's some swelling there .
- [History of Present Illness] uh , you have some swelling there and some redness . uh , you have some pain with flexion , extension of your elbow as well .
- [History of Present Illness] so we'll try some motrin , uh , about 800 milligrams every six hours . uh , i'll give you a sling for comfort , just so you can use it if- if it's causing a lot of pain .
- [History of Present Illness] so we'll give you a sling , we'll give you the motrin , i'll give you about , uh , 30 , uh , uh , 30 , uh , uh , medications for that .
